featuresSubtitle: Big image files slow everything down. We make them smaller without making them look worse. Simple as that.
feature1Desc: Your images get 50-80% smaller. A 5MB photo might become 1MB. Same picture, way smaller file. Websites load faster, uploads finish quicker.
feature2Desc: Got 20 photos that need compressing? Upload them all together. We'll handle them all at the same time and give you everything back in a ZIP file.
feature3Desc: We use smart compression. Your eyes won't spot the difference between the original and compressed version, but the file size difference is huge.
feature4Desc: Everything's encrypted when you upload. We compress your images and then delete both the originals and compressed versions after an hour. Nobody sees your stuff.
feature5Desc: JPG from your camera, PNG screenshots, WEBP graphics - we handle all of them. Whatever image format you've got, we can make it smaller.
feature6Desc: Smaller images mean pages load in a flash. Good for your visitors, good for Google rankings. If you run a website, this is a must-have tool.
stepsSubtitle: Compress your images in under a minute
step1Desc: Drop your JPG, PNG, or WEBP files. Add up to 20 images if you want to compress a whole batch.
step2Desc: Our system figures out the best way to shrink each image without wrecking the quality. Takes just seconds.
step3Desc: Grab your compressed images one by one or download them all in a ZIP. Done deal.
faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about image compression
faq1Q: Will my photos look worse after compression?
faq1A: Nope. We use smart compression that keeps your images looking sharp. You probably won't even notice the difference, but your file sizes will drop by 50-80%. Text stays readable, colors stay vibrant, details stay crisp.
faq2Q: What kinds of images can I compress here?
faq2A: JPG, PNG, and WEBP - those are the big three. Pretty much any photo or graphic you've got will work. Phone photos, screenshots, graphics from Photoshop, whatever.
faq3Q: Can I do a bunch of images at the same time?
faq3A: Yeah, up to 20 at once. Great for when you've got a whole folder of photos that need shrinking. Upload them all, we'll compress them all, you download them all in one ZIP file.
faq4Q: Why should I bother compressing images anyway?
faq4A: Smaller files mean faster websites, quicker uploads, less storage space eaten up. If you're putting images on a website, compressed versions load way faster. Your visitors won't sit there waiting for huge photos to appear.
faq5Q: What happens to my images after I compress them?
faq5A: We delete everything after an hour. Your originals, the compressed versions, all of it. We encrypt the upload process too, so nobody can peek at your photos while we're working on them.
seo1Title: When You Need to Compress Images
seo1P1: Big image files are a pain. They take forever to upload, eat up storage space, and make websites crawl. If you've ever waited for a webpage to load because some huge photo is taking its sweet time, you know what I'm talking about.
seo1P2: That's where compression comes in. We squeeze the file size down - way down - without making your images look bad. A 10MB photo becomes 2MB. Still looks great, but now it loads fast and doesn't hog space.
seo2P: Smaller files load faster on websites. Google likes fast websites, so compressed images help your SEO. They're easier to email - nobody wants to wait while a 50MB attachment uploads. They use less cloud storage. They're friendlier to people on mobile data plans. Basically, smaller is better for everyone involved.
seo3P: Website owners compress images so pages load lightning fast. Photographers shrink portfolios for online galleries. Marketers optimize graphics for email campaigns and social posts. Bloggers compress photos before uploading to WordPress. Anyone who deals with images regularly needs this tool in their back pocket.
ctaSubtitle: Join thousands who use SmallPDF.us to compress images every day. Free, fast, no account needed.
